name: Red Hot Grilled Potatoes
recipeIngredient: 12 small New white potatoes, about 2 1/4 lb total
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400 degrees. Place rack in center of oven. Prepare a large, foil-lined baking pan.
2. Place potatoes in a plastic food bag. In a small dish, combine olive oil, paprika, chili powder, cayenne pepper, and salt. Add the mixture to the bag with the potatoes. Seal the bag and shake well to coat the potatoes evenly.
3. Transfer the coated potatoes in a single layer to the prepared baking pan. Drizzle any remaining oil and spices over the potatoes.
4. Bake until just barely tender (but still firm), about 25 to 30 minutes, depending on the size of the potatoes. Turn the potatoes occasionally during baking. The baked potatoes can be kept at room temperature several hours before grilling.
5. To serve, sprinkle with salt and place over hot barbecue fire for a few minutes, turning often until seared and hot.
These potatoes are "red hot" from the paprika, chili powder, and cayenne pepper. Omit the pepper if you prefer a milder taste.
